disclaimer: Entry rules can change. Always verify with official immigration or embassy sources before booking.

visa.notes: Dutch ordinary passport holders can visit Türkiye visa-free for tourism or transit for up to 90 days within any 180-day period. No e-Visa or prior application required — entry is granted at the border. Passport must be valid for at least 6 months beyond the planned departure date.
visa.verify_note: Confirm current visa-free status at the Turkish Ministry of Foreign Affairs (mfa.gov.tr) or your country's foreign ministry website before travel. Rules can change.

insurance.notes: Not required but strongly recommended. Turkish private hospitals are excellent and widely available, but costly without insurance. Recommended if hiking Cappadocia, hot air ballooning, or visiting remote areas.

regions.notes: Extremely crowded and hot in Jul–Aug. Spring and autumn are perfect for sightseeing.
Hot air balloon flights best Apr–Oct. Snow in winter can close roads.
Peak beach season Jul–Aug is extremely hot (40°C) and crowded. Shoulder months are ideal.
High altitude regions are snowbound Nov–Apr. Best visited in spring or early autumn.

budget.verify_note: Turkish Lira has experienced significant inflation. Rates may differ substantially — check xe.com immediately before travel.

budget.label: Backpacker
budget.includes: Dorm bed, local eateries 3x, bus/tram, 1 tea

mid.label: Comfort
mid.includes: Budget hotel or cave hotel, restaurants, local transport

high.label: Boutique
high.includes: Cave hotel in Cappadocia, restaurants, hot air balloon, private tours

useful_apps.name: BiTaksi
useful_apps.use: Main taxi app in Turkey — metered, safer than street hails

useful_apps.name: Uber
useful_apps.use: Available in Istanbul — uses licensed taxis

useful_apps.name: Google Maps
useful_apps.use: Navigation and transit — Istanbul's public transport is well mapped

useful_apps.name: XE Currency
useful_apps.use: Lira rates fluctuate — keep this open during your trip

transport: Istanbul has excellent public transport: metro, tram, ferry and bus. Get an Istanbulkart (refillable transit card) at any metro station — cheaper than single tickets.
The Marmaray tunnel connects European and Asian sides under the Bosphorus by rail.
Ferries across the Bosphorus are scenic and cheap — a staple of daily Istanbul life.
BiTaksi or Uber for taxis — avoids meter disputes common with street taxis in tourist areas.
Intercity overnight buses (Flixbus, Metro Turizm) are comfortable and affordable — book at obilet.com.
Domestic flights: Turkish Airlines and Pegasus cover the country cheaply if booked early. Cappadocia (Nevsehir or Kayseri airports) is the most common route from Istanbul.

scams.name: Shoe shine drop scam
scams.description: A man drops his shoe shine brush near you. When you pick it up he offers to shine your shoes for free, then demands a high fee. Classic Istanbul scam — just walk on.

scams.name: Restaurant menu swap
scams.description: Tourist-area restaurants in Istanbul (around Sultanahmet and Taksim) show a cheap menu outside then present a different one inside. Check prices before sitting.

scams.name: Bar / club bill shock
scams.description: In Beyoğlu and beach resorts, complimentary drinks arrive then appear on the bill. Or bills are inflated. Only go to well-reviewed establishments and always ask for an itemised bill.

scams.name: Grand Bazaar 'friendly tea' upsell
scams.description: Extremely friendly shopkeepers offer tea and conversation — a classic soft sales technique. Enjoy the hospitality but never feel obligated to buy.

embassy.phone: Verify official embassy contact before travel
embassy.address: Verify official embassy contact before travel

emergency.notes: 112 is the universal EU-style emergency number in Turkey and works on all mobile networks. Tourist Police in Istanbul are based near the Blue Mosque in Sultanahmet.

checklist.item: Passport valid 6+ months beyond return date
Confirm current visa-free status at mfa.gov.tr or your country's foreign ministry website
Book hot air balloon ride in Cappadocia well in advance (fills up 2–3 months ahead)
Download Google Maps offline for Istanbul, Cappadocia and Antalya
Notify bank — inform them of TRY transactions
Buy Airalo eSIM or get a Turkcell/Vodafone Turkey SIM at the airport
Pick up an Istanbulkart at the airport or metro (if spending time in Istanbul)
Book overnight bus or domestic flight for Cappadocia early in peak season
Be aware of dress code when visiting mosques — cover head, shoulders, knees
